texts.full_text: - Polar or neither could be correct if it doesn't dissolve in water either. But since you said "dissolves ...", the conclusion should likely be: - The substance is probably polar. --- Q#29: Oxygen will most likely form an ionic compound with sulfur, as both are electronegative elements: - Sulfur can gain electrons (forming ions like SO²⁻). - Oxygen can lose or share electrons easily. * Calcium and Hydrogen tend to react in other ways; Chlorine doesn't usually bond ionically. --- Q#30: Potassium would produce a similar violent reaction with water due to its high reactivity: * It reacts violently producing hydrogen gas, heat (even causing the solution/water temperature rise). - Mg & Cr may have some reactions but are not as reactive; S is typically insoluble in H₂O. --- Q#31: The number of moles can be calculated using volume and molarity: \[ \text{Moles} = V_{solution}(\frac{\text{liters}}{L}) × M(\frac{mole}{l})(for a 500. mL solution, first convert to liters: ) \] 3.18 mole ≈ correct answer. --- Q#32: Chemicals should not be mixed together haphazardly: * Pour waste chemicals into the appropriate container/separate containers. - Don't mix incompatible substances (e.g., strong oxidizing/reducing agents). After, follow lab protocols/disposal
